title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet to play their first game at TD Garden against Montreal Victoire on Saturday night.
The game is sold out, expected to be one of the highest-attended in PWHL history with over 17,800 fans.
Fleet features Olympic athletes like Megan Keller and Aerin Frankel, who have bolstered the team's success.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, of Fall River pleaded guilty to leading a sex trafficking ring involving two minors and one adult.
Parker coerced an adult victim into sex work and recruited minors using false promises; she faces up to life in prison.
Parker and co-defendants trafficked victims in Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and South Carolina; one defendant still awaits trial.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Police found two dead dogs in Weymouth, a terrier-type and a Pitbull mix, discovered on different days.
The Pitbull mix was found wrapped in a plastic bag, prompting suspicion and a public request for information.
Both dogs lacked identification tags or microchips, hindering police efforts to locate their owners for closure.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her son, Micah, who died from necrotizing enterocolitis.
Canvasser founded the NEC Society, a nonprofit dedicated to preventing and caring for NEC, after her son's death.
Following the marathon, Canvasser will present on NEC care at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting in Boston.

title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, from Braintree, sentenced to life without parole for 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel.
Carey was indicted in 2022 after DNA evidence linked him to the murder; found guilty of strangling Gravel to death.
Gravel's body was found near Route 128 in Beverly; her case went cold until developments in 2012 revived it.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, has completed his first practice with the Boston Bruins, aiming to make his NHL debut soon.
Coach Marco Sturm plans to evaluate Hagens's fit in the team before deciding his debut, emphasizing caution and readiness.
Hagens, a former Boston College player, is expected to boost Bruins' offense, potentially enhancing power play and scoring depth.

title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: 76ers center Joel Embiid will undergo surgery for appendicitis, missing Thursday's game against the Rockets.
Embiid's absence is uncertain, impacting the 76ers' playoff position as they currently hold the seventh seed.
Embiid is averaging 26.9 points per game over 38 games this season, missing two of four matchups with the Celtics.

title: State commits $4M to address substance use crisis at Mass. and Cass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: State commits $4 million to address substance use crisis at Boston's Mass. and Cass area.
Funding allocated includes $2.24 million for housing, plus support for clinical staff and diversion programs.
Opioid crisis efforts led by Mayor Wu show a 38% drop in opioid-related deaths in Boston.

title: Family of Stephenson King Jr., the man killed by Boston police officer now charged with manslaughter, demands transparency
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Family of Stephenson King Jr. demands release of police body-camera footage after his fatal shooting by Officer Nicholas O'Malley in March.
Attorney Ben Crump calls for transparency and accountability at press conference, highlighting King's struggles with mental illness.
Officer O'Malley, charged with manslaughter, pleads not guilty and receives support from fellow police; GoFundMe raises over $500,000.

title: Her first marathon is for her dad and grandfather — lifelong South Boston residents
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Olivia Dennett, 22, is running her first Boston Marathon to honor her family and support South Boston Neighborhood House.
Dennett's grandparents founded Joseph's Bakery in 1967, a key South Boston community hub; her grandfather passed away last March.
Running for 'The Ollie' symbolizes her family's legacy in South Boston, fostering community support and family connections.
